title: A New Way to Help Maine’s Fishing Industry
subtitle: A unique program is using donated fish to support local fishermen and feed hungry students.

summary.en: Maine’s fishing industry is facing serious financial challenges due to high fuel costs and inflation. A program called Fishermen Feeding Mainers is helping by buying local fish and donating it to schools and food banks. This initiative provides a steady income for fishermen while offering healthy food to those in need. It also introduces young students to local seafood, which may help build future demand. The program relies on private donations and government funding to keep operating.
summary.zh: 緬因州的漁業因高昂的燃料成本與通貨膨脹，正面臨嚴峻的財務挑戰。「漁民餵養緬因人」計畫透過收購在地魚貨並捐贈給學校與食物銀行來提供協助。這項倡議不僅為漁民提供了穩定的收入，同時也為有需要的人提供了健康的食物。它還讓年輕學生接觸到在地海鮮，這可能有助於建立未來的市場需求。該計畫目前依賴私人捐款與政府資金來維持運作。

paragraphs.en: The fishing industry in Maine is currently facing a difficult time. Many fishermen who catch groundfish—species that live near the bottom of the ocean, such as cod and haddock—are struggling to make a living. According to NPR Business, rising costs for fuel and food have made it very hard for these workers to keep their businesses running. The situation became much worse during the COVID-19 pandemic when many restaurants closed, causing the market for local fish to disappear almost overnight.
paragraphs.zh: 緬因州的漁業目前正處於艱難時期。許多捕撈底棲魚類（即棲息在海底附近的魚種，例如鱈魚和黑線鱈）的漁民正為了生計而掙扎。根據美國公共廣播電台（NPR）商業新聞報導，燃料與食品成本的上漲，使得這些工作者難以維持營運。在新冠肺炎疫情期間，情況變得更加惡劣，當時許多餐廳被迫歇業，導致在地魚貨的市場需求幾乎在一夕之間消失。

paragraphs.en: To help solve this, a special program called Fishermen Feeding Mainers started in late 2020. This program raises money from private donors, grants, and government funds to buy fish directly from local fishermen. Once the fish is bought, it is processed into fillets, frozen, and given away for free to schools and food banks across the state. NPR Business reports that the program has already spent more than $4 million to buy about 1.3 million pounds of fish.
paragraphs.zh: 為了協助解決此問題，一個名為「漁民餵養緬因人」（Fishermen Feeding Mainers）的特別計畫於二〇二〇年底啟動。該計畫透過私人捐款、補助金與政府資金籌集經費，直接向在地漁民購買魚貨。魚貨收購後會被加工成魚片並冷凍，隨後免費提供給全州的學校與食物銀行。NPR 商業新聞報導指出，該計畫已投入超過四百萬美元，購買了約一百三十萬磅的魚貨。

paragraphs.en: For fishermen like Devyn Campbell, who works in Boothbay, this program has been a necessary lifeline. Before it started, he often faced very low prices for his catch at auctions. When the market price for a type of fish drops, the Maine Coast Fishermen’s Association uses the program’s money to buy the fish before the price falls too low. This gives fishermen more security because they know they will not lose money on their hard work, even when the market is unpredictable.
paragraphs.zh: 對於像在布斯貝（Boothbay）工作的戴文·坎貝爾（Devyn Campbell）這樣的漁民來說，這項計畫已成為必要的救命稻草。在計畫啟動前，他常面臨漁獲在拍賣會上價格極低的情況。當某種魚類的市場價格下跌時，緬因州海岸漁民協會（Maine Coast Fishermen’s Association）會利用計畫資金在價格跌得太低前買下魚貨。這為漁民提供了更多保障，因為他們知道即使市場難以預測，自己的辛勤工作也不會血本無歸。

paragraphs.en: This initiative does more than just support the fishing crews; it also helps the community. Many schools and food banks in Maine have trouble buying fresh fish because of rising prices. According to NPR Business, retail seafood prices went up significantly at the end of 2025. Because of this, many institutions simply cannot afford to put fish on the menu. The program provides a free, healthy source of protein to these groups. In fact, some school districts have been willing to drive for more than 10 hours to pick up the frozen fish for their students.
paragraphs.zh: 這項倡議不僅僅是支持漁船作業，也對社區有所助益。由於物價上漲，緬因州的許多學校與食物銀行在購買新鮮魚貨時面臨困難。根據 NPR 商業新聞報導，海鮮零售價格在二〇二五年底大幅上漲。正因如此，許多機構根本無力將魚類列入菜單。該計畫為這些團體提供了免費且健康的蛋白質來源。事實上，有些學區甚至願意開車超過十個小時，只為了替學生領取這些冷凍魚貨。

paragraphs.en: At first, some people were worried that children would not enjoy eating fish. Mary Hudson, the director of fisheries programs at the Maine Coast Fishermen’s Association, noted that parents and teachers were hesitant at the start. However, those worries disappeared once the students actually tried the meals. Hudson explained that the children really like the fish. Since October 2020, the association has donated more than 1.8 million meals to various organizations, with over 200,000 meals provided just last year.
paragraphs.zh: 起初，有些人擔心孩子們不會喜歡吃魚。緬因州海岸漁民協會的漁業計畫主任瑪麗·哈德森（Mary Hudson）指出，家長與老師一開始持保留態度。然而，當學生們實際嘗試這些餐點後，這些擔憂便消失了。哈德森解釋說，孩子們真的很喜歡這些魚。自二〇二〇年十月以來，該協會已向各個組織捐贈了超過一百八十萬份餐點，僅去年一年就提供了超過二十萬份。

paragraphs.en: Looking toward the future, the program aims to do more than just provide immediate help. By serving fish in schools, the organization is teaching a new generation of children to enjoy local seafood. Hudson believes this will create future markets for Maine’s fishing industry. As these students grow up and start buying food for their own families, they will already be familiar with the local catch. This long-term goal is a key part of why the program continues to seek support.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，該計畫的目標不僅僅是提供即時的幫助。透過在學校供應魚類餐點，該組織正在教導新一代的孩子喜愛在地海鮮。哈德森相信，這將為緬因州的漁業創造未來的市場。當這些學生長大並開始為自己的家庭採買食物時，他們對在地漁獲早已不陌生。這個長期目標是該計畫持續尋求支持的關鍵原因之一。

paragraphs.en: Despite these successes, the industry still faces many challenges. Inflation—the general increase in prices over time—continues to make life difficult for everyone in the state. Fishermen like Campbell are still dealing with the high costs of operating their boats. While the program has been a great success, it remains a temporary solution to a much larger economic problem. For now, however, it offers a way to keep the boats on the water and the food banks stocked during these tough times.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管取得了這些成功，該產業仍面臨許多挑戰。通貨膨脹（即物價隨時間普遍上漲的現象）持續讓州內每個人生活艱難。像坎貝爾這樣的漁民仍需應對高昂的漁船營運成本。雖然該計畫取得了巨大成功，但對於一個更龐大的經濟問題而言，它仍然只是一個暫時性的解決方案。然而，在這些艱困時期，它確實提供了一種讓漁船繼續出海、讓食物銀行物資充足的方法。

paragraphs.en: In conclusion, the Fishermen Feeding Mainers program shows how creative thinking can help solve complex economic issues. By connecting the needs of local fishermen with the needs of schools and food banks, the program has created a system that helps everyone involved. It keeps the local economy moving, provides healthy meals to children, and ensures that Maine’s fishing heritage can survive even when the market is struggling. As the program enters its sixth year, it remains an important example of how communities can work together to face economic pressure.
paragraphs.zh: 總結來說，「漁民餵養緬因人」計畫展現了創意如何協助解決複雜的經濟問題。透過連結在地漁民的需求與學校及食物銀行的需求，該計畫建立了一個造福所有參與者的體系。它維持了在地經濟的運作，為孩子們提供了健康的餐點，並確保緬因州的漁業傳統即便在市場低迷時也能存續。隨著該計畫進入第六個年頭，它依然是一個社區如何共同合作以面對經濟壓力的重要典範。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A thing on which someone or something depends or which provides a means of escape from a difficult situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 救命稻草、生命線（指在困境中提供關鍵幫助的事物）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常指物理上的救生索，這裡比喻為經濟上的援助。文中：For fishermen like Devyn Campbell, who works in Boothbay, this program has been a necessary lifeline.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To manage, operate, or be in charge of a business or organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「跑步」，這裡指經營事業。文中：According to NPR Business, rising costs for fuel and food have made it very hard for these workers to keep their businesses running.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To keep a supply of something available for use.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（庫存、股票），這裡作動詞，指維持物資供應。文中：For now, however, it offers a way to keep the boats on the water and the food banks stocked during these tough times.

sources.name: NPR Business
sources.title: One solution for Maine's struggling fishing industry? Give fillets away for free

quiz.question: What is the primary function of the Fishermen Feeding Mainers program when market prices for fish fluctuate?

options.A: It forces restaurants to buy the fish at higher prices.
options.B: It buys the fish from fishermen before the market price drops too low.
options.C: It sells the fish at auctions to help fishermen make a profit.
options.D: It provides fuel and food subsidies directly to the boat owners.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，當魚類市場價格下跌時，Maine Coast Fishermen’s Association 會利用該計畫的資金在價格跌得太低之前買下漁獲，這為漁民提供了保障。選項 A 錯誤，因為該計畫是將魚提供給學校和食物銀行，而非強迫餐廳購買；選項 C 錯誤，因為該計畫是為了避免漁民在拍賣時面臨低價，而非透過拍賣獲利；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到漁民面臨燃料與食物成本上漲，但計畫本身是直接購買漁獲，而非提供燃料補貼。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the long-term impact of the program on Maine's fishing industry?

options.A: It aims to replace all commercial seafood sales with government-funded donations.
options.B: It expects that students will eventually become consumers of local seafood as adults.
options.C: It believes that providing free fish will stop the rise of inflation in the state.
options.D: It intends to make fishing a more profitable career than other industries in Maine.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第六段，該計畫透過在學校供應魚類，讓新一代孩子習慣食用當地海鮮，Hudson 認為這將為未來的漁業創造市場，因為這些學生長大後會更傾向購買當地漁獲。選項 A 過於極端，文章並未提及要取代商業銷售；選項 C 錯誤，文章最後一段明確指出該計畫只是暫時性的解決方案，無法解決通貨膨脹等宏觀經濟問題；選項 D 錯誤，文章未提及要讓漁業比其他產業更具獲利性。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'heritage' mean in the context of 'Maine’s fishing heritage'?

options.A: The current financial profit of a business.
options.B: The legal laws regarding ocean boundaries.
options.C: The traditional culture and history passed down through generations.
options.D: The physical equipment used to catch fish.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。Heritage 在此處指「遺產」或「傳承」，代表一個地方長期累積的文化、歷史與傳統。文中提到該計畫幫助 Maine 的漁業傳統（fishing heritage）得以延續。選項 A 指的是獲利，與文化傳承無關；選項 B 指的是法律規範；選項 D 指的是設備，這些都無法表達 heritage 在文化與歷史層面的意涵。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the Fishermen Feeding Mainers program?

options.A: It is a permanent government solution to fix the high costs of fuel and food.
options.B: It focuses exclusively on improving the nutritional habits of school children.
options.C: It demonstrates how community collaboration can address economic challenges and support local industries.
options.D: It highlights the failure of the private sector to support local fishermen during the pandemic.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章總結段落明確指出，該計畫展示了創意如何解決複雜的經濟問題，透過連結漁民與學校、食物銀行的需求，創造出互助的系統，這正是全文的核心主旨。選項 A 錯誤，文章強調這只是「暫時性」的解決方案；選項 B 太過狹隘，忽略了對漁民經濟支持的重點；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未將重點放在指責私部門，而是強調透過捐贈與補助來進行合作。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
